claims.claim: KPMG launched a periodic CapEx/OpEx benchmark across key DC markets but no NL-specific PDU or fit-out cost figure surfaced
claims.sourceTitle: Benchmarking CapEx and OpEx in the global data centre market
claims.excerpt: this paper launches a periodic benchmark for DC capital expenditure (CapEx) and operating expenditure (OpEx) across key markets, highlighting regional cost variation and its underlying drivers.

tensions.text: Netherlands hyperscale (self-build) data center market is forecast to grow at 26.41% CAGR (2026-2032) versus the Netherlands colocation market's 3.54% CAGR (2023-2029) and the overall NL data center market's 9.10% CAGR (2026-2031) — implying self-build capacity is growing several times faster than colocation, which could erode the colo-share basis (951 MW colo / 3,451 MW CE-7) used to derive geo.NL's 0.28 ratio if self-build MW is not proportionally represented in the CE-7 colo denominator.

No PDU-vendor or BOM dataset splitting rack-PDU revenue/units per installed colo MW by country (NL vs CE-7) was found, so the underlying premise of the query — that PDU-euro intensity per MW could confirm or refute NL's 0.28 apportionment — remains untested by any surfaced source; the two evidentiary sources for geo.NL (Statista, Dutch Data Center Association) are colocation-capacity counts, not spend-intensity data, so no independent corroborating or contradicting signal on 'euro intensity per MW' exists in this batch.

absences: No vendor-level (Vertiv/Legrand/Schneider) PDU revenue or unit-shipment split by country (Netherlands vs CE-7 average) was found — the core requested dataset does not appear to exist in accessible form.
No colo fit-out bill-of-materials (BOM) dataset showing discrete rack-PDU spend per MW for the Netherlands compared to Germany/France was found.
No figure quantifying 'PDU-euro intensity per MW' for any country was located; all PDU market sizing found is at the pan-European or global level, not country-disaggregated.
The 6Wresearch Netherlands Rack PDU report exists as a purchasable product but the retrieved excerpt shows only import-country competition (HHI) framing, not a per-MW revenue or intensity metric.

No data distinguishing whether Netherlands hyperscaler self-build capacity is served by different PDU vendors/procurement channels (bypassing typical colo fit-out BOM) than colocation capacity was found, despite this being central to the group's 'self-build hollowing' framing.
